tables.Notable inhabitants: Drow outposts, goblins, kobolds, cave fishers
Aboleth, elder evils, deepspawn, umber hulks

body:
# FR — Regions Complete Reference (D&D 3.5)

Sources: FRCS (2001), Silver Marches (2002), Unapproachable East (2003), Shining South (2004),
Serpent Kingdoms (2004), Lands of Intrigue (1997)
Live: https://forgottenrealms.fandom.com/wiki/[Region_Name]

Timeline anchor: 1372 DR. All present-tense describes 1372 DR situation.

---

## THE SWORD COAST AND THE NORTH

### Waterdeep (The City of Splendors)
Government: Hidden Lords (secret oligarchy) + Open Lord (Piergeiron the Paladinson)
Population: ~132,000 (city proper); up to 1.3M in greater area
Races: Primarily human; significant elf, dwarf, halfling presence
Climate: Temperate coastal
Key districts: Castle Ward (government), Dock Ward (crime/trade), Sea Ward (wealth),
North Ward (nobles), Trade Ward (commerce), Southern Ward (working class), City of the Dead
Notable organizations: Force Grey (city militia elite), Watchful Order of Magists and Protectors,
Masked Lords, Lords' Alliance, most major guilds
Beneath the city: Undermountain (megadungeon, 23+ levels, Halaster Blackcloak); Skullport
(pirate haven in Undermountain level 3)
Regional feats: Waterdhavian Noble (social access), Arcane Accompaniment

### Baldur's Gate
Government: Grand Dukes (4) elected by a merchant council (Parliament of Peers)
Population: ~42,000
Races: Predominantly human
Economy: Major trade hub; river trade + sea trade. Iron throne (merchant consortium).
The Flaming Fist mercenary company enforces law (often corruptly).
Notable areas: Upper City (wealthy), Lower City (poor, dangerous), Outer City (travelers)
Regional feats: Street Smart

### Neverwinter (The City of Skilled Hands)
Government: Lord Nasher Alagondar (warrior-king, effectively)
Population: ~23,000
Races: Human majority; strong gnome artisan community
Economy: Famous for fine craft goods, especially clocks and plumbing. River trade.
Notable: Three bridges span the Neverwinter River. Nearest major city to Thundertree ruins.
Mount Hotenow volcano nearby (active). The Host Tower of the Arcane (Luskan mage guild) nearby.
Regional feats: Discipline

### Luskan (City of Sails)
Government: High Captains (5 pirate lords ruling by consensus and threat)
Population: ~16,000
Economy: Piracy, northern trade, harsh commerce
Notable: The Arcane Brotherhood (powerful mage guild) headquartered at Host Tower.
Relationship with Neverwinter: Often hostile.
Regional feats: Arcane Schooling (Arcane Brotherhood)

### The Silver Marches (Luruar)
Government: Loose confederation of city-states; Alustriel of Silverymoon primary ruler
Member cities: Silverymoon, Everlund, Sundabar, Quaervarr, Citadel Adbar (dwarven)
Population: Mixed; human, elf, dwarf
Climate: Cold northern
Notable: The High Forest nearby. Constant orc/goblinoid pressure from the north.
Silverymoon: Most cosmopolitan city in the North; famous for its library (Vault of Sages) and
Spellguard; ruled by Alustriel (one of Mystra's Chosen). The Moonbridge is magical.
Regional feats: Silver Palm, Arcane Schooling (Silverymoon)

### The North (Wild Lands)
Includes: Spine of the World mountains, the Evermoors, Delimbiyr Vale, Nether Mountains
Major threats: Orc hordes (Kingdom of Many-Arrows under Obould Many-Arrows), frost giants,
trolls of the Evermoors, undead of Hellgate Keep ruins
Important: The Underdark below this region connects to Menzoberranzan and other drow cities.

---

## THE HEARTLANDS

### Cormyr (The Forest Country)
Government: Monarchy; King Azoun IV died 1371 DR. His daughter Alusair serves as regent for
infant Azoun V in 1372 DR.
Population: ~1.3M
Races: Human (Cormyrean) primary; some elves and dwarves
Capital: Suzail | Key cities: Arabel, Eveningstar, Tilverton (now ruined by shadow magic)
Military: War Wizards (crown battle mages) + Purple Dragons (royal army)
Economy: Trade, farming, light industry. Strongly mercantile with laws.
Notable: The Stonelands (troubled northern border with goblinoids). Haunted Halls (dungeon near
Eveningstar). Curse of the Azure Bonds events are historical.
Regional feats: Cormyrean Conscript, Militia (Cormyr)

### Sembia
Government: Oligarchy of merchant lords; no single ruler; council rules by economic influence
Population: ~1.5M; densely settled
Capital: Ordulin | Major cities: Selgaunt, Saerloon, Urmlaspyr, Yhaunn
Economy: Richest trading nation in interior Faerûn; coins minted here accepted everywhere
Notable: Semberholme (ruin of ancient elven city nearby). No public temples (taxes, not worship).
Sembia replaced Myth Drannor trade routes.

### The Dalelands
Government: Each Dale is semi-independent, ruled by a Dalesfolk council or chosen leader
Major Dales: Shadowdale (home of Elminster), Mistledale, Battledale, Deepingdale, Featherdale,
Harrowdale, Tasseldale, Archendale, Dagger Dale, Scardale
Climate: Temperate, forested
Notable: Cormanthor Forest borders the Dales to the south; Myth Drannor ruins within.
The Dale Council meets rarely; the Dales are bound by the Dalelands Compact.
Shadowdale: Population ~1,200; home of Elminster Aumar (Sage of Shadowdale, Chosen of Mystra).
Tower of Ashaba is the historic seat of the lord.
Regional feats: Forester

### The Western Heartlands
Includes: Boareskyr Bridge, Sunset Mountains, Secomber, Hill's Edge, Triboar, Longsaddle
Notable: Trade Way runs through here connecting Baldur's Gate to the North. Numerous adventuring
ruins. The Graypeak Mountains contain goblinoid tribes. Fireshear (mining town) to far north.

---

## THE MOONSEA AND SURROUNDS

### The Moonsea
Government: Individual tyrannies (each city ruled by a different tyrant or council)
Cities: Phlan (recovering from undead invasion), Melvaunt (iron trade), Hillsfar (humans only by law),
Mulmaster (Blade Tyrant rules; most powerful single city), Zhentil Keep (Zhentarim headquarters;
partially ruined, being rebuilt), Yûlash (ruined)
Climate: Cold; harsh winters
Notable: The Moonsea itself is cold and dark. Ruins of ancient empires abound.
Constant warfare, treachery, and political instability. The Zhentarim dominate trade routes.

### Zhentil Keep
Government: Zhentarim Black Network; Fzoul Chembryl (High Priest of Bane) rules 1372 DR
Population: ~50,000 (recovering from 1368 DR destruction)
Notable: The Black Road trade route links Zhentil Keep to the east and west.
Manshoon (once ruler; now several clone bodies scattered across Faerûn after a clone war).

### The Vast
Cities: Ravens Bluff (city of adventure), Tantras (port, once held Tablet of Fate)
Government: Varies by city; Ravens Bluff is democratic-leaning
Notable: Popular adventuring ground.

---

## THE FAR NORTH

### Anauroch (The Great Desert)
Climate: Harsh desert (magical in origin — created by the Phaerimm draining the land)
Notable: Ruins of the empire of Netheril scattered throughout. The Bedine tribes live here.
Shade Enclave: The returned Shadovar city-state. The Netherese mage princes of Shade returned
after ~1,700 years. They control the Anauroch and seek to restore Netheril. Their primary
resources include shadow magic and artifacts from the old empire. Major threat as of 1372 DR.
The Phaerimm: Life-draining creatures imprisoned beneath Anauroch (released by the Shadovar
removal of the mythallar — this happens in the novels but is a 1372+ event).

### Icewind Dale
Location: Beyond the Spine of the World; Ten-Towns area
Population: Sparse human settlements (Ten-Towns) + dwarf clan Battlehammer (Bruenor's clan)
Climate: Arctic; brutal
Notable: Kelvin's Cairn (mountain). Reghed barbarians live on the tundra.
Crenshinibon (Crystal Shard) — artifact associated with this area (Drizzt lore).

---

## THE UNDERDARK (UNDER-FAERÛN)

### Major Cities and Regions

**Menzoberranzan** (menz-oh-BEAR-an-zan)
Government: Eight ruling drow matron mothers (House Baenre dominant)
Population: ~20,000 drow + ~10,000 slaves of other races
Location: Northdark (beneath Silver Marches/North)
Notable: House Baenre is unquestionably dominant after the novel events (1358 DR+).
Bregan D'aerthe mercenary band (Jarlaxle's drow) operates from here.
Key families: House Baenre, House Barrison Del'Armgo, House Mizzrym, House Fey-Branche,
House Tuin'Tarl, House Do'Urden (destroyed), House Hunzrin, House Fey-Branche

**Blingdenstone** (BLING-den-stone)
A svirfneblin (deep gnome) city; partially destroyed; survivors fled; a plot hook in 1372 DR.

**Gracklstugh** (GRACK-el-stoog)
Duergar (gray dwarf) city. The Deepkingdom. Stoneheart Enclave (duergar theurges) and
Clan Cairngorm (stone giants) notable factions.

**Ched Nasad** (SHED nah-SAD)
Drow city built of calcified webs. Second major drow city after Menzoberranzan.

**The Lowerdark**
Below the Upperdark and Middledark; home of aberrations (aboleth, beholders, illithid, elder evils).

---

## THE SOUTH AND WEST

### Amn (The Merchant's Domain)
Government: Council of Five (merchant lords, one from each major guild/family)
Capital: Athkatla ("City of Coin") | Other cities: Murann, Purskul, Nashkel, Trademeet
Population: ~3M (densest in Faerûn)
Races: Human majority; halflings common
Economy: Wealthiest nation; controls the cloud giant enclave trade routes south.
Notable: Magic is taxed and regulated. Spellcasters must be licensed. Shadow Thieves guild.
Maze of Shadows (thieves' guild labyrinth). The Underdark connects beneath.

### Tethyr
Government: Restored monarchy; Queen Zaranda Star Rhindaun rules 1372 DR
Capital: Saradush | Region of recovery from civil war
Notable: Recovering from decades of civil war. Many refugees. The Wealdath (forest) in south.

### Calimshan (The Land of the Sword)
Government: Pasha of Calimport (nominally); actually controlled by genasi noble families
Capital: Calimport (population ~190,000 — largest city in Faerûn by population)
Other cities: Memnon, Almraiven, Manshaka
Races: Human (Calishite); djinn bloodlines (air genasi) in noble class; former slaving empire
Climate: Desert coast
Notable: Deepest history of any nation in Faerûn — former Shoon Empire, djinn overlords.
Enormous criminal underbelly in Calimport (many tunnels beneath the city).

### Chult (Peninsula)
Government: Tribal / Yuan-ti dominated areas / Port Nyanzaru (independent city-state)
Climate: Tropical jungle; extremely dangerous
Races: Human (Chultans), Yuan-ti, dinosaurs, undead (Tomb of the Nine Gods area — not yet 3.5)
Notable: Dinosaurs roam freely. Heavy jungles. Cursed ruins of Mezro (empty city; returned
in some novels). Port Nyanzaru is main civilized outpost.
Regional feats: Chultan Jungle Fighter

### Halruaa
Government: Magehocracy; Halruaan Mages rule all aspects of life
Population: ~2.5M
Races: Human (Halruaan — descendants of Netherese)
Climate: Warm mediterranean
Notable: Extremely xenophobic. Powerful wizards. Airships (halruaan skyships) fly everywhere.
Zulkirs have no role here — that's Thay. Halruaa uses a different magical tradition.

### Luiren (Halfling Homeland)
Government: Clans led by clan elders + Warmaster Sheelagh (military leader)
Population: ~1M (half of all halflings)
Climate: Subtropical
Races: Halfling (Ghostwise and Strongheart)
Notable: Only place in Faerûn where halflings truly rule themselves. Dense farming communities.

### The Shaar
Geography: Great grassy plain in southern Faerûn
Inhabitants: Shaaryan nomads, gnolls, various humanoid tribes
Notable: Ancient ruins of the Shaaran Empire. Monsters aplenty. Lions, elephants.

---

## THE EASTERN HEARTLANDS AND BEYOND

### Thay (The Land of the Red Wizards)
Government: Council of Zulkirs (8 archmages, one per school of magic)
Capital: Eltabbar | Other cities: Bezantur, Amruthar, Tyraturos, Delhumide, Tyraturos
Population: ~4.5M (mostly slaves and peasants)
Races: Human (Mulan) rulers; gnolls as military; slaves of all races
Climate: Temperate-warm
Notable: Red Wizards tattooed and shave heads. Zulkir Szass Tam (lich, Necromancy) is the
de facto most powerful but cannot formally control all. Thay establishes enclaves (trade posts)
across Faerûn to sell magic items at discount (a long-term strategy).
The Red Wizard prestige class (FRCS) originates here.
Military: The Griffon Legion (aerial cavalry), Clay Golem armies, gnoll slave soldiers.

### Aglarond
Government: The Simbul (Witch-Queen, one of Mystra's Chosen, Alassra Silverhand) rules.
Population: ~1.3M
Races: Human, half-elf (high percentage of half-elves due to The Yuirwood elf presence)
Climate: Coastal temperate
Notable: The Yuirwood forest in the interior shelters wild elves. The Simbul's power keeps
Thay at bay. Active conflict with Thay for over a century.

### Rashemen
Government: Theocracy; Witches (Hathrans) rule; the Iron Lord is a ceremonial warrior-king
Population: ~1.1M
Races: Human (Rashemi); very insular
Climate: Cold northern
Notable: One of the strongest female magic-using societies. Berserker warriors (Rashemi othlor).
The Wychlaran (Hathrans) are druid/wizard hybrids who wear masks. Vremyonni (male wizards)
are secret and hidden. Immense spirits (spirits of the land) protect Rashemen.
The Hathran prestige class is exclusive to Rashemen females.

### Thesk
Government: Council of towns; trade-focused; no unified ruler
Population: ~700,000
Races: Human (Theskian); orc-mixed (half-orc) common due to the Orc Wars
Notable: The Way of the Dragon (trade road) passes through Thesk connecting east and west.
Has made peace with some orc tribes who now integrate into society.

### The Unapproachable East
Includes: Thay, Aglarond, Rashemen, Thesk, Altumbel, Great Dale (druid stronghold)
General: Frontier region, heavily contested, ancient powers. East of the Easting Reach.
The Great Dale: Druids of the Nentyarch (powerful druid circles) maintain forest equilibrium.

---

## THE INNER SEA COAST

### Turmish
Government: Assembly of Stars (democratic)
Population: ~1.6M
Races: Human (Turmian) majority; halflings common
Climate: Mediterranean
Capital: Alaghôn | Known for: Trade, olive oil, crafts
Notable: Strongly supports Silvanus worship. Relatively peaceful.

### Chessenta
Government: City-state confederation; each city-state under its own tyrant or council
Population: ~1.2M
Climate: Temperate
Notable: Gladiatorial combat is culturally central. City-state of Luthcheq is ruled by a
wizard-hating fanatic. City-states war constantly. Descended from Unther.

### Unther
Government: Theocratic despotism under Gilgeam (god-king); dying empire
Population: ~1.5M; collapsed under Mulhorandi pressure
Climate: Warm dry
Capital: Unthalass (Unther City)
Notable: Gilgeam was (is) a god; killed Tiamat's avatar during Time of Troubles but was wounded.
Mulhorand is invading/has invaded. Unther is in terminal decline in 1372 DR. Genasi (fire)
are common here. Many refugees fleeing to Chessenta.

### Mulhorand (The Empire of the Gods)
Government: Divine theocracy; each of the major gods is an actual deity-descended ruler
Population: ~3M
Races: Human (Mulan) + genasi (notably sand genasi)
Capital: Skuld | Other: Gheldaneth, Beluir
Notable: The ruling family of each department traces lineage to an actual deity avatar. The most
stable government in Faerûn. Annexed Unther in 1372 DR. Strong arcane tradition.

---

## ISLAND REALMS AND ISOLATED NATIONS

### The Moonshae Isles
Government: High King Derid Kendrick + individual island kings
Races: Ffolk (human Celtic analog), Northmen (Norse analog), Llewyrr (moon elves)
Climate: Cold oceanic
Notable: Druidic circles (Earthmother worship). Conflicts between Ffolk, Northmen, and
occasional giant/hag threats. The Korinn Archipelago is more dangerous.

### Evermeet (The Green Isle)
Government: Queen Amlaruil Moonflower
Races: Elves only (gold, moon, sea). Non-elves require invitation.
Location: Far west in the Trackless Sea
Notable: The elven homeland in Faerûn. Physically connected to Arvandor. Highest concentration
of elven magic anywhere. Visiting is nearly impossible without elven escort.

### Lantan
Government: Council of Artificers
Races: Human (Lantanese) + gnomes
Notable: Worships Gond primarily. The most technologically advanced nation. Gnomish
inventions and firearms (gonne) experimented here. Ships powered by wind-catchers.

---

## THE UNDERDARK REGIONS (Summary)

| Region | Location below | Notable inhabitants |
|---|---|---|
| The Upperdark | 0–3 miles down | Drow outposts, goblins, kobolds, cave fishers |
| The Middledark | 3–10 miles | Drow cities, duergar, illithid, beholders |
| The Lowerdark | 10+ miles | Aboleth, elder evils, deepspawn, umber hulks |
| The Northdark | Under the North | Menzoberranzan, N'Tel'Quess (non-drow settlements) |
| The Southdark | Under the South | Drow outposts, deep dragons |
| The Buried Realms | Below Mulhorand | Ancient ruins, genies |

---

## REGIONAL FEATS (Player's Guide to Faerûn)

Each character from a specific region may take 1 bon Regional feat at character creation
(in addition to standard feats). These require the specific birthplace/upbringing.

| Region | Sample Regional Feats |
|---|---|
| Aglarond | Simbul's Blade, Luck of Heroes |
| Amn | Street Smart, Silver Palm |
| Calimshan | Deft Hands, Smooth Talk |
| Chessenta | Warrior's Valor, Tactical Insight |
| Chult | Chultan Jungle Fighter, Serpent Blood |
| Cormyr | Cormyrean Conscript, Militia (Cormyr) |
| Dalelands | Forester, Alertness |
| Dragon Coast | Mercantile Background, Smooth Talk |
| Halruaa | Magical Artisan, Spellwise |
| Impiltur | Forester, Militia (Impiltur) |
| Luruar (Silver Marches) | Silver Palm, Arcane Schooling |
| Moonshaes | Ffolk Heritage, Northman Heritage |
| Mulhorand | Blooded, Heroic Destiny |
| Rashemen | Rashemi Elemental Affinity, Blooded |
| Sembia | Mercantile Background, Cosmopolitan |
| The Shaar | Shaaryan Mounted Combat, Track |
| Thay | Tattoo Focus, Arcane Schooling |
| The North | Survivor, Track |
| Unther | Survivor, Warrior's Valor |
| Waterdeep | Waterdhavian Noble, Cosmopolitan |
| The Western Heartlands | Militia (various), Survivor |

Full list (50+ regional feats) in Player's Guide to Faerûn (2004).
